nativeRange.habitat: Shallow reef environments, rubble zones, and hard substrate in tropical marine waters

imageCandidates.notes: Product image from Mosaic Macros listing for Botryocladia occidentalis; shows characteristic grape-cluster bladder form

summary: Red Grape is a Caribbean red alga (Botryocladia occidentalis) with clusters of fluid-filled, grape-like bladders on branching stems — a sought-after display specimen for reef aquariums with moderate lighting.
careNotes:
Red Grape (Botryocladia occidentalis) is a Caribbean red macroalga generally well-suited to small and mid-size reef tanks. Standard reef parameters are advised: temperature 22–26°C, pH 8.1–8.4, salinity SG 1.023–1.025, calcium 400–450 ppm, and magnesium 1250–1350 ppm. Nitrate is generally advised in the 5–20 ppm range and phosphate 0.02–0.1 ppm to support healthy growth. Medium, full-spectrum lighting tends to suit this genus; actinic-only spectra have been observed to be insufficient and are generally not recommended. Flow should be moderate — conditions similar to a soft-coral tank; strong direct flow can tear and dislodge the delicate bladder clusters.

WARNING — SAND BED PLACEMENT: As a red alga, B. occidentalis will melt at the base if buried in a sand bed. It must be attached to hard substrate only — via superglue, zip-tie or fishing line, wedging between rocks, or on a plug secured to rockwork. Do not place in an open sand bed.

Growth is generally slow compared to green macroalgae, making it well-suited to display without aggressive pruning. Nutrient export capacity is considered low given the slow growth rate. Weekly dosing of iron, iodine, and other trace elements is generally recommended to support long-term health. Fragmentation is the primary aquarium propagation method — healthy thallus sections can be detached and re-attached elsewhere. The fluid-filled bladders may burst if handled roughly or grazed, releasing spores; it is generally advised to keep the specimen away from aggressive grazers and to remove dislodged fragments promptly. No documented allelopathy. A sibling species, B. skottsbergii (Red Bubble, sw-algae-079), has a smaller, rounder bubble form and broader Indo-Pacific distribution.
breedingNotes: In the aquarium, propagation is typically achieved by fragmentation — detaching a healthy thallus section and re-attaching it to rock via superglue, zip-tie, or wedging. Bladder rupture can also release spores that may establish secondary growth under favorable conditions.

primary.name: Mosaic Macros
primary.notes: Primary hobby trade source for this species. Confirms grape-cluster bladder morphology, Caribbean/western Atlantic origin implied by species epithet, and display placement on plug. Sold as 2–3" or 3–4"+ cutting.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros — Macroalgae Care Guide
additional.notes: Slice-wide secondary source. Provides authoritative parameter targets: temp 22–26°C (72–78°F), pH 8.1–8.4, Ca 400–450 ppm, Mg 1250–1350 ppm, NO3 5–20 ppm, PO4 0.02–0.1 ppm. Explicitly states red algae will melt at base if placed in sand beds; must attach to rock only.
